title: Behavior Tests, Type Tests, and Safe Refactoring · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: Evolve a public API while behavior, diagnostics, inferred types, and downstream consumers remain intentionally compatible. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a behavior-and-type regression suite with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests.
Write stable rejected-usage tests that fail when diagnostics disappear or move for the wrong reason.
Build changed-schema, invalid-value, async, and clean-consumer fixtures around public boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"6" that must not enter the trusted model.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
